data.title: As a DSO maintainer, I verify the installer-template interface contract
data.description:
## Description

What: Audit scripts/create-dso-app.sh against the delivered template to confirm they agree on every interface surface. (New story from adversarial review F5.)
Why: The installer was merged assuming a template shape; this epic builds the template. Without an explicit contract-verification step, template-done and installer-works may pass separately while diverging.
Scope:
- IN: Written interface contract (as a comment block in create-dso-app.sh or a doc at docs/designs/create-dso-app-template-contract.md) enumerating: (a) template repo URL match (line 226); (b) every sed/substitution pattern in the installer has a corresponding placeholder in the template and vice versa; (c) post-clone steps (tickets branch fetch, plugin_root write, npm install) match template layout; (d) tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh fixture expectations align with template.
- OUT: Installer rewrite; template structural changes (those belong in S2/S3 and must be fixed there if the contract audit surfaces them).

## Done Definitions

- When complete, a written interface contract exists at a documented path enumerating all four surfaces (URL, placeholder set, post-clone steps, fixture expectations).
  Satisfies: cross-cutting defense-in-depth for SC2, SC3, SC5
- When complete, any divergence discovered during the audit is either fixed in-PR (if trivial) or filed as a separate bug with a pointer recorded in this storys closing comment.
  Satisfies: SC7 defense-in-depth
- When complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] The contract document must be updated whenever either the template or the installer changes its interface. Consider adding a pre-commit or CI check that flags divergence.

## Escalation Policy

Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue - one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
